author: luca_zanolini
content: Fair, the drift isn't free-form. The next subsection ('Block status') should make it precise: bid.parent_block_hash of any canonical block always equals the block_hash of the most recent FULL ancestor, so the two pointers can only differ by skipping consecutive EMPTY blocks. The two-pointer framing is meant to set up what's actually stored on-chain. We can certainly make this binary nature explicit since the beginning tho

author: potuz
content: Thing is that when thinking about the security properties and assumptions, it is a very common misconception even among client devs implementing this, that parents are kinda arbitrary, when in fact a block pin points a single ancestry chain which is very restrictive, you can't pick up a random pair of canonical (root, hash) and choose it as your parent. This misconception, while it may be very simple to you guys, I'm pretty certain that takes time to absorb cause I've seen it countless times, so it's better to avoid it right away

author: luca_zanolini
content: Yes, I see your point. Just to make sure I’ve understood you: Given the parent beacon block, the bid.parent_block_hash isn't a free choice: it's forced to equal state(parent).latest_block_hash (asserted in process_execution_payload_bid). So a builder doesn't choose two pointers independently; they choose one (which beacon block to extend), and the execution parent follows deterministically from the parent's state. The two pointers framing seems to suggest more freedom than the protocol actually allows. Is this what you mean?

author: potuz
content:
exactly this. But rather than getting into the technical issue of what is a *parent state* which for a block would not contain the latest block hash part yet due to 5094. The two options are better framed as follows:

A block chooses the parent block. Then in that parent block there is a bid, that has two hashes: a parent hash or a block hash. These are the only two possible parent hashes for the incoming block.

author: luca_zanolini
content: That’s a great framing. Want to open a PR or should I go ahead and fix it?

author: potuz
content: What in that repo? No way... I want to make it finally to bed and between Discord and slack I'm not allowed 🤣
